input.question: Compare the environmental, economic, and social impacts of electric vehicles versus internal combustion engines.
partial_answers: Electric vehicles produce zero tailpipe emissions but battery manufacturing has significant mining costs.
EVs have lower operating costs while ICE vehicles have lower upfront prices.
EVs reduce noise pollution in cities but the transition raises equity concerns for lower-income drivers.

expected.final_answer: Electric vehicles produce zero tailpipe emissions, reducing urban air pollution and greenhouse gases, but their battery manufacturing has significant mining and energy costs. Economically, EVs have lower operating costs while ICE vehicles have lower upfront prices and established infrastructure. Socially, EVs reduce noise pollution and health impacts in cities, while ICE phase-outs raise equity concerns for lower-income drivers who cannot yet afford the transition.
